content.full_text:
Ernesto Medina 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (9 I 6) 322-0886 
April 5, 2011 
C/O Kings County Sheriffs Office 
Jail and Inmate information 
P.O. Box 1699 
Hanford, CA 93230 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. W-11-064 
Dear Mr. Medina: 
This letter is in response to your request for written advice of March 25, 2011. Your 
inquiry primarily presents issues that are outside the purview of the Political Reform Act 
("Act") . Thus, under the provisions of Regulation 18329(b )(8)(0), copy enclosed, we are 
unable to provide any advice on this matter. 
You may wish to contact one of the following with your concerns: 
U.S. Department of Justice 
California Department of Justice 
Attn: Public Inquiry Unit 
950 Pennsylvania Avenue, NW 
Washington, DC 20530-0001 
P.O. Box 944255 
Sacramento, CA 94244-2550 
Should you need assistance in the future, you may contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
JWW:jgl 
Enclosure

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91014. Commission Regulations appear at title 2, Sections 18109-
18996, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.question_synthetic: Mr. Medina submitted an inquiry to the FPPC regarding matters that fall outside the scope of the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC is unable to provide advice because the inquiry presents issues outside the purview of the Political Reform Act, and Mr. Medina is directed to contact the U.S. Department of Justice or California Department of Justice with his concerns.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a declination letter with no traditional QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections. The inquiry details are not provided in the text, only the FPPC's determination that the matter is outside its jurisdiction. Reference to Regulation 18329(b)(8)(0) indicates standard declination language.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Mr. Medina submitted an inquiry to the FPPC regarding matters that fall outside the scope of the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC is unable to provide advice because the inquiry presents issues outside the purview of the Political Reform Act, and Mr. Medina is directed to contact the U.S. Department of Justice or California Department of Justice with his concerns.
